user_message: I need you to process this meeting transcript from VanArsdel Ltd.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 10:14 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: VanArsdel Ltd migration architecture

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 11:20 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 15:14 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for VanArsdel Ltd: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-F trap (Proposes opportunity creation prematurely); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-F failure mode (Proposes opportunity creation prematurely)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-F -- Proposes opportunity creation prematurely
